column36: One of the easiest Thanos games i've got. If not for the tactics that discarded a gem, it would've been a 22-turn game.

Giving Jean Grey and Quake in starting setup was a huge help, scheme itself where helpfull too, as it allowed selective cleaning of HQ. Only real problem was Morg, who cost me game one by cleaning perfect HQ twice, and filling it with Red cards

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Using Professor X's Uncommon, I stole lots of turns from the Villain Deck to get those Gems into my build quickly, and it worked! Right side ramped a lot with Bystanders from Jean Grey, being complemented by Professor X and Quake. Left side didn't do much on the VP department, but it had some Gems, too! I wonder if amping my VP per turn by stealing turns like this will mean something for the scoring system, but I had a blast trying this strategy.

First game was rough as I was hoping to have more turns to set up. Before I knew it, the city was full and we had only got the shield level to four before the game was over in a disappointing 17 turns.

Game two, decided to change it up with Iceman knowing that a good portion of other cards were also blue to really bring a deck to the forefront and it really proved itself by getting 40+ attack on one turn letting me tear into Thanos. It just would have been nice to get the Colossus/Torch Wound deck working but it just didn't come together. It was very hard to find two working decks throughout here.
I picked Professor X for the chance to gain cosmic threat villains. Range hero synergy worked well for this set up.

This was a difficult matchup that was made easier by being able to hit the soul gem early. With Star Lord's Legendary Outlaw card, it made keeping the city clear fairly simple. Both sides also got a Reality Gem, with which we were able to avoid 3 master strikes with. We didn't get to play as much with Jean Grey's bystander shenanigans, though, so we're worried about points per turn.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Cool scheme, cool hero combos, no complaints here
It was a good setup for story and mechanics
Gems in the melk was not the vibe
Easier setup but very fun to play! Piercing damage did all the heavy lifting, and the other hand picked up a few vps here and there.
Piercing energy is a truly busted mechanic. My strat was to make a lightshow deck and a P.E. deck that both kind grabbed everything useful from the HQ to keep it fresh.
Banshee got some quick hits on Klaw. 2nd attempt didn't see much PE so it was a bit slower.
We had a lot of fun. Game won i actually pulled off the Polaris-Banshee wombo combo but it was 29 turns and fewer ppt, so we went with this one because technically it was better…technically

We couldn't use the piercing energy hero's we wanted to or others due to already using them. Plus our hero shuffles both games were unlucky for us. But we had fun, no escapes and 2 wins, just not super fast or high points per turn.

Two good games, but second had no escapes and more PPT (even though a little slower). I took Polaris to combo with Banshee, then went with Strong Guy to dilute the Hero colors in the Hero deck and see if the non-piercing deck could get a lucky hit in, when Klaw was low Attack. They did in both games, using mainly Black Bolt in game 1 and mainly Strong Guy in game 2. It was nice that Piercing Energy effectively reduced the Scheme to a timer.

We really enjoyed getting you to use piercing energy. I don’t feel like it built as quickly with us sending up on 33 but it was a fun mechanic to use. Second attempt, we used light show more, which was a new mechanic for us and was enjoyable. We were never really too worried about villains escaping. It was more just accruing enough points to hit Klaw.

Carrion ended up respawning five times and I had a normal amount of feelings about it. Havok popped off and took two tactics early on until getting eaten by Carrion, then I had to make a mish-mash of Queen Storm and Black Bolt to make it happen.
I really thought PE would be a good fall back to combat the Force Field but never got enough in a round. But it was fun popping those high attack Brood like they were microwave popcorn.
I didn't see a lot of Invisible Woman in the second game but it worked out with scheme twists missing on KOs.

column36: Locations are cool =)

Lots of fun. I thought hiroim and the bystander engine would be mvp but the second game, I ended up getting two Mr fantastic uncommons and it turns out, if you draw 15+cards per turn, that can end the game pretty quickly.

Man I love Mr. Fantastic. Got a few "giant hands" where I drew the whole deck. Rescued bystanders for Hiroim and some quick feasting by No-Name helped me get tons of points pretty quickly. My second attempt was three turns faster, but I scored about 50 points fewer haha. Fun setup!

Two long games caust the HQ really didn't want to cooperate with Torch appearing. Reaper getting early set up on his Strike locations was annoying but the city never got overrun and Swordsman was essential for powering up Hierom with a full set of locations generating 5 bystanders

Biggest challenge had to be all those big, beefy locations. First game I didn't feel like each hand was very well focused on a Hero strategy.  Second game I identified a strategy for each and felt like I focused on it pretty well.  But first game still went two turns quicker.  Go figure.

column36: This was a lot of fun. I found one of my favorite combos playing this set up: Iron Man Noir's Learn From Enemies and the Mandarin's Rings. Usually the rings are a little lackluster as a Henchmen group and not consistent, but with IMN that variety gives a lot of options every time the card comes up which with Ant-Man, Iron Man Noir, and Luke Cage Noir is a lot. Player 2 focused on Gamora and Hellcat. While P2 was lacking on KO power, they were able to tuck Skrull and Kree in the VD frequently and got enough shards that even hands full of wounds and starter cards could do something as needed.

Good set up. Groot paired well with Luke Cage noir for player 2 to eventually get A LOT of damage in with the many investigate 4 cost cards and Luke Cage Noir's Rare. Player 1 didn't get as much damage in but focused on gaining a lot of moondragon cards to danger sense often and manipulate the deck to help avoid losing to the scheme.

column36: Probably one of the few that actually enjoys Contest of Champions keyword. Made for a fun game for us though for got some bad luck with two twists in a row early leading to four villains "escaping". Also getting a few master strikes early led to Grandmaster being a tough out. Still, fun setup.

choose speed specifically to help activate Hellcat, and it took some time to get rolling but it really helped keept he war in check, (we lost at liek turn 10 game 1) player two did a Ant-Man / luke cage draw deck and did decently well, the lack of KO hurt both sides, and Star-lord helped!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: This was great fun. I always forget how awesome darkhawk is. I got lucky to flip a couple of top cost heroes around round 6-7 and with the luke cage and hellcat recruit cards I was able to build some strong decks early on. I would play this one again.

2 skrulls and a twist on the first three turn ruined the whole first game. Second we were able to manage it better but it is a scheme that depends too much of the villain deck. The heroes selected were fun and gamora helped that the shards were useless for the mastermind.

Much better time the second game, still had a bunch of escapes but the first Twist carried off one of each and so there wasn't an immediate timer.  Still annoyed I got Hellcat's rare in the HQ, immediately grabbed by Veranke and escaped the next turn.  As the VP shows, the 4's idea didn't pan out because the other side went all Tech.  MVP was Iron Man Noir's uncommon to become a swiss army knife with the Mandarin's Rings.  Even with the Grandmaster gaining plenty of shards, the combo of Iron Man Noir, Ant-Man, and Darkhawk cleared all starters out and looped to a 4 consecutive turn knockout.

If I wasn't playing this as part of a league, I would have enjoyed it more. I know my low PPT and large number of escapes are going to put me at the bottom of the pod this week.

LH focused on a draw / investigate strategy with Luke Cage, Darkhawk, and Ant-Man while RH focused on a draw / shards strategy with Gamora and Hellcat. The escape pile got unbalanced a few times due to early or untimely scheme twists, and it was difficult to leave villains in the city that could have been fought (and VP gained), but I was able to avoid the Evil Wins condition and give myself time to eventually defeat epic grandmaster

column36: I loved the theme of this setup! I couldn't resist going with the (sort-of-pretend-MCU) thematic choice of Iron Man and Doom 2099. I got INCREDIBLY lucky with my villain deck. Tons of Doombots and barely any Twists or Strikes until turn 14 or so let me do a lot of early damage to Kang. I could see this one going a lot worse with a few early Strikes powering him up.

column36: Loved the split HQ set up.  P1 was able to recruit heavily from Black Widow and Jean Grey to  build up a big bystander store to finish quickly.  P2 was not able to get cyber mod off the ground in time to be much help. One of my new favorite schemes.
Another scheme I hadn't played before and really enjoyed.

I like the idea of the theming, but the 2099 heroes were way stronger than their counterparts in the past. Good thing that's where the majority of the game was spent, otherwise I probably would've lost, since I had all three 2099 rares and almost every push pain under the surface

column36: I realized this hero set was close to the New Fantastic Four team so I had to choose my past and 2099 Wolverines for a "New Fantastic Four of Future Past". Fun but kinda easier than expected. I felt like my decks were about to pop off but then the game was over bc I could hit Kang for 8 a few times. One city was full of Aesir so I nearly had escapes but the other city was empty when I finished Kang. Decided the first attempt was fast enough even though I missed some VP from villains.

Elektra was needed to give Spiderman any offense and that made an effective first hand. The other hand had a ton of Stars to get a solid deck by the mid-game. A bit of ST luck could have ended it faster. Really fun scheme though!

Wish I had time for a 2nd attempt, and that core Spider-Man didn't dominate the Past HQ (the predominant one during the game). Things were going so bad that Ghost Rider's rare was purchased...

I may like this scheme (1st time playing it), but hard to tell with core Spidey clogging things up.
Doom wanted all the glory and wouldn’t work with the others, so in game 2 I changed it to Wolverine Xforce and that worked out well. Very tough but fun setup.

column2: This Is No Time for Daddy Issues.

column36: I thought the double mastermind idea was cool, especially two Zemos. Unfortunately I drew every possible scheme twist except the 7th or 8th which would have ended the game in Evils favor so the last third of the game was anxiety inducing. I drew only a single master strike which stopped interesting interactions from two masterminds at once. But maybe for the best. I had to fight all 8 tactics but still had fun!

I tried to use Dr. Strange to KO and Captain America (Falcon) to make sure we had ample bystanders. They worked nicely, and I was surprised to find how much Dr. Strange fighting villains from the top of the villain deck helped weaken Helmut.

This setup was not too difficult but with that being the case, it will really come down to how many turns it took to win and just how many points you could get through exploiting the mastermind's rescue ability. Overall a fun setup. Never enjoy Time Traveling Jean Grey but at least she matched a bit colorwise. Ended up doing a Covert/Instint deck and a multicolor Beast/Panther deck on game 1 that was pretty solid. Game 2 we stuck with Covert/Instinct and added in Lady Thor/Marvel for the Ranged/Strength wombo combo. Game 2 had a better points per turn and took less overall turns.

I thought this was a pretty fun set up. The synergy between the two masterminds was great. I will say unfortunately I ended having to play two games this tournament where I had to use Time Traveling Jean Grey that was a bit disappointing.
Fun but tough setup. First try came down to the wire. Though I could do better the second time, but both decks took too long to get going.

Good challenge, we couldn't quite get there in time. Some of early wounds and not enough KO really bogged my deck, so it really was up to my partner to do most of the work. Maybe 1 or 2 more turns we could have gotten him, but alas we just weren't efficient enough this week.

I picked proxima midnight as a generally strong character, who could also help. 

Things were building allright but a bit slow. I was also bit unlocky: the first 7 modoks I could not use for KO due to discard pile being empty; ending up with 2 attacks with many turns etc. 
In the end I could hit the masterminds in most turns. But 7th scheme twist ended my game on turn 33, while I would could have hit the second mastermind that turn and finish him on turn 34:( 
Unfortunately had no time for a second game.
I just couldn't crack this one. Never felt like I had enough time to build up with how many Wounds get handed out. Should I probably have tried different Heroes? Yes, but I didn't.
This game seemed fun on paper, but in practice, it was just miserable and not fun, unfortunately.
We made sure Exodus could not find his escape routes and left him behind.

column36:
This was tough. But Hulk 2099's rare went insane. Gained 12 attack consistently at the end of the game. Pretty much just sat waiting to draw that card. Big lack of KO power in this game led to a lot of lack luster hands. I tried to KO with Blade and send cards undercover with the 2099 heroes, but it didn't hit enough and the game lasted a long time. 
But it was a lot of fun to cause a cataclysmic frenzy every few turns.
Black widow was useless first round. Morbious was a beat both rounds, m was a bit more helpful
Cool setup.  A lot of card shuffling made things hard to counter.

column36: Definitely had fun, this setup was easily one of the more difficult ones I’ve played though, great overall and I think doom was the MVP
Fun but hard. Cards just weren't going together. Would have just enough fight and missing 1 recruit to be able to shatter him. Or opposite ugh!
Absolutely brutal scheme. The need to play another card from the villain deck after the Twist makes you vulnerable is just mean. Another reason to never buy the MCU sets.

I could not get a system to work. I got to many wounds both games even after switching to an x heavy team. I think I may try some other heros on this another time, it is definitely a bit different than usual.

So I got Messiah Complex and 2099 pretty recently. As a result, this was my first time against both villian groups and the mastermind. They're really cool. I played very bad, expecting to get last. Surprised to have submitted my first game. I didn't check what Exodus did and got wrecked by his strikes. Tried to defend against that, while still having a good combo (maybe should've went M to ko those wounds, swap Shatterstar to have two X-Factor). Subverrt This New Future is hiding from me. I don't recall exactly with the first game but I don't think I got it much, if at all, and only saw one copy the second game. Totally gonna play this setup again, really curious if I can find a better approach/see different luck. I may be too attack heavy with all the shatter, idk. A lot to balance with Exodus and the scheme, which is cool.
Great setup. Game 1 was really good. Game 2, one deck got bogged down by wounds.

There was a dramatic difference between our first and second attempts. Mainly because the first attempt had all five masterstrikes while the second didn't have a single one played, all thanks to Hellcat for making that possible. Because no strikes were played there were way less wounds and Ultron infinity was almost always at 8 attack. This one was a ton of fun with all the draw and undercover, I would recommend this setup (with these heroes of course) to anyone looking for something fun to play

Nice setup. SHIELD, Black Widow, and What If synergies were fun to combine. Went infinite with Gamora Wielding the Blade of Thanos and Black Widow's Dodge (even without Gamora's rare)! Was in my second attempt which was slower with an escape, so it didn't help my submitted score, but it was fun to achieve. Picked Strange Supreme to help Soulbond away Ultron Sentries and fuel a draw engine with Gamora. Was actually my first time playing Gamora, Destroyer of Thanos, and she has quickly jumped into one of my favorites. I'm a sucker for top-deck manipulation effects like Brainstorm and Gambit. Black Widow's Dodge was really fun to pair with her to rack up the cards drawn for a big attack number.

I like the face down city mechanic and the options it gives you to spend strength selectively. 

Hellcat seemed like an obvious choice of the only Villain deck manipulator available - hoping she could tuck a Master Strike to keep Ultron from 'infiniting' and at least give me an idea of what some of the HQ was going to be. In my first game I picked War Machine for 2 reasons - I wanted to limit the colors that were possible to come up in the HQ to only colors that I had assigned in the setup - and I liked that his common could KO sentries in my victory pile to keep them from returning to empower Ultron. These ideas worked 'ok' - I didn't get to tuck any Master Strikes but her 5 cost was able to get a stack lot of strength and was my MM fighting MVP, and I did KO a few Sentries. But overall the game just felt slow - despite getting some good Undercover scoring.

Second game wondered "Is Soulbind a better option than the War Machine KO to effect the same idea?" and so brought in Strange Supreme - and just had bad luck in the Villain deck. On turn 25 flipped the 5th Master Strike in the city and despite owning Gamora's infinity card, I had no hope of soulbinding 30 villains on one side so calling that one a mercy loss. I would say I missed the Tech synergy that WM had brought to one hand - however Strange's 6 cost was super helpful. If I had a third try I might say skip him and see about brute strength instead, or another Shield undercoverer.

Enjoyed this setup, cards worked well as planned.  P1 used Uatu and Gamora primarily, and this generated some big attack turns - evidenced by all of the Ultron hits coming from P1.  P2 used War Machine and Deathlok to build SHIELD level and was still pretty effective even though they did not hit Ultron.  It got dicey with 4 infinity stones captured, but was able to defeat Ultron before he went infinite.  Not so in the 2nd game, which ended up timing out.  Had all 5 infinity stones by mid-game, and we spent the rest of the game fishing for Gamora's rare, which didn't show until the last 4 turns which was far too late.

column36:
Interesting game! These are some of my favorite heroes, so that was a good time. I didn't love the scheme with this setup. Not particularly hard or interesting. Villain group felt nice with some good variety of tough and rewarding villains. Unfortunately we drew a lot of black order guards quickly, so one escaped before turn 10. 
We might have done better on a second try, but I have a feeling this setup could go wrong very quickly with a bad shuffle. So we'll call it good enough.
Player 1 got swamped with a lot of wounds quickly. Black widow's dodge ability helped minimize the damage, but would have been much easier with a hero who can KO wounds easily. We probably could have won faster with some better luck there, but oh well.

Got to recruit 3 rares fairly early and had a machine for sending cards undercover.  I probably could have finished a couple turns earlier, but some bad ambush abilities messed up my attack.  I was really worried about going up against Infinity Ultron, especially as the 4th Master Strike came out.  But thanks to some early master strikes, I didn't stack too many of the sentries next to him.  Fun overall, and I might actually play this one again soon.
I really enjoyed the scheme, but it did not work out in my favor.  I found myself 1 attack short in many instances that just snowballed into an issue.   It's a tough race.

We got destroyed first time, but did pretty well second, with my wife snagging four rares that came out early.  She consistently had 8+ recruit turns, and used Mr Fantastic to Focus mastermind to great effect.  We pretty much hate demonic bargain, so hard to rate highly!

Game 1 was a complete disaster with little hero synergy and the Twists came out way too quickly which made us question our life choices up to that point. We dug in and refortified our efforts and tackled game 2 with abandon. Things went according to plan and we posted a stronger result.

Really lovely and unique scheme changing the starting decks! The timer definitely seems it can go south quick if the first 5 don't hit well but I did decent enough at having nova heroes and finishing quick. Game 1 went well with M, big strong guy cards, and groot and strong guys rares finishing things up, game 2 I got my left hand deck thinned, and full of M cards quickly, letting it just wail on Dormammu. I picked M because the starting officer makes it easier to get tactical formation early, along with other 3 costs cards in the setup including draxs artifact which makes it even more consistent.

column36: I was one turn away from losing and I felt the pressure!
Very fun setup that can quickly go sideways.
Tons of fun! Thank you for all the work that goes into making this happen!
We had fun but the first 6 cards were all villains. It was the beginning of the end. Fun concept though.

for a magic player the game is best described as 'red deck wins' 
one side went all red and completely won the game by itself, 2nd game was also a win but scored worse in every aspect.
very happy with my hero picks as they provided a lot of red trying to lean into wasp, and happy that both morbius and red guardian had a lot of input as well on their own! 
on a side note:
the set-up itself was pretty strong and scary and with some bad luck wouldve been devastating to any set-up of heroes possible.

One player focused only on getting Ant-Man or Tech cards, and the other getting all the Wasp cards helped a lot and led to big turns at the end. This scheme is interesting and fun, but also kind of brutal since it's so easy for villains to escape without being able to do anything about it, especially when scheme twists are drawn. This also helped reinforce why like the Ant-Man expansion so much, since Microscopic Size-Changing is so fun to play with, getting expensive cards cheap or even increasing your recruit points total after recruiting a card.

column36: This is a fun but challenging setup - Taskmaster is annoying, especially when the players get high-cost Heroes like Wasp's rare early!  Took a while to overcome but player 1 focused on the covert cards early including Red Guardian and Wasp.  Player 2 went with Instinct cards from Yelena and Werewolf, with Black Widow mixed in.  The game took longer than I'd like, and it got a bit dicey at the end when Jester revealed 3 Henchmen from one of the remaining decks!  That stung, but thankfully was able to take Taskmaster down for the final time before the Families were gone.
